title: digitalis purpurea var. carnea
intro: Digitalis purpurea var. carnea, also known as Foxglove Pink, is a variety of the common foxglove plant, a biennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant contains cardiac glycosides, particularly digoxin and digitoxin, which have been used for centuries in the treatment of heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, due to its ability to increase the force of the heart's contractions and slow the heart rate. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea var. carnea have been used to treat a range of health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced blood pressure, and alleviation of symptoms associated with heart failure and arrhythmias. The herbal preparations of Digitalis purpurea var. carnea typically involve the leaves, which can be dried, powdered, or extracted into a tincture or ointment, and should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
